mnemonics: D.C.P. Police: Decentralized Control Points to the Police Chief (Centralized) to settle bus arguments (arbitration).
keyPoints: Bus arbitration resolves conflicts when multiple devices request bus access simultaneously.
There are two main methods: Centralized (using a single arbiter) and Distributed (each device has control logic).
Common arbitration protocols include Daisy-Chaining (priority-based), Polling (rotating priority), and Independent Requesting (parallel requests).
commonMistakes: Confusing bus arbitration (access control) with bus multiplexing (data routing).
Assuming a daisy-chain configuration implies a fair, round-robin priority system when it is fixed.
